---
title: "Chocolate Sales Dashboard"
format: dashboard
scrolling: True
page-layout: full


execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

df = pd.read_csv("chocolate_sales_clean.csv", parse_dates=["Date"])

monthly = (
    df.groupby("Month Label", as_index=False)
      .agg(Sales=("Amount", "sum"),
           Boxes=("Boxes Shipped", "sum"),
           Orders=("Amount", "size"))
      .sort_values("Month Label")
)

country = (
    df.groupby("Country", as_index=False)
      .agg(Sales=("Amount", "sum"),
           Boxes=("Boxes Shipped", "sum"),
           Orders=("Amount", "size"))
      .sort_values("Sales", ascending=False)
)

product = (
    df.groupby("Product", as_index=False)
      .agg(Sales=("Amount", "sum"),
           Boxes=("Boxes Shipped", "sum"),
           Orders=("Amount", "size"))
      .sort_values("Sales", ascending=False)
)

salesperson = (
    df.groupby("Sales Person", as_index=False)
      .agg(Sales=("Amount", "sum"),
           Boxes=("Boxes Shipped", "sum"),
           Orders=("Amount", "size"))
      .sort_values("Sales", ascending=False)
)

yearly = (
    df.groupby("Year", as_index=False)
      .agg(Sales=("Amount", "sum"),
           Boxes=("Boxes Shipped", "sum"),
           Orders=("Amount", "size"))
      .sort_values("Year")
)

yearly["Growth %"] = yearly["Sales"].pct_change() * 100

yearly_avg_growth = yearly["Growth %"].mean()

total_sales = df["Amount"].sum()
total_boxes = int(df["Boxes Shipped"].sum())
total_orders = len(df)
avg_order = df["Amount"].mean()
num_countries = df["Country"].nunique()
num_persons = df["Sales Person"].nunique()
num_products = df["Product"].nunique()
top_country = country.iloc[0]["Country"]
top_product = product.iloc[0]["Product"]

```

## Row  {height=150px}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Total Sales"
dict(
    color="primary",
    value=f"${total_sales:,.0f}"
)

```


```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Total Orders"
dict (
    icon = "pencil-square",
    color = "primary",
    value = f"{total_orders:,}"
)
```


```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Salesmen"
dict (
    icon = "people-fill",
    color = "primary",
    value = num_persons
)
```
## Row {height=150px}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Average Yearly Growth"
dict (
    icon = "bar-chart",
    color = "primary",
    value = f"{yearly_avg_growth:.2f}%"
)
```



```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Number of Countries"
dict (
    icon = "globe2",
    color = "primary",
    value = f"{num_countries}"
)
```



```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Number of Products"
dict (
    icon = "",
    color = "primary",
    value = f"{num_products}"
)
```

## Row {height=300px}

```{python}
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_bar(x=yearly["Year"], y=yearly["Sales"], name="Sales")
fig.add_scatter(x=yearly["Year"], y=yearly["Boxes"], name="Boxes", yaxis="y2", mode="lines+markers")
fig.update_layout(
    title="Yearly Sales and Shipment Volume",
    xaxis_title="Year",
    yaxis=dict(title="Sales ($)"),
    yaxis2=dict(title="Boxes", overlaying="y", side="right"),
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=60, b=20)
)
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.show();
```

## Row {height=300px}

```{python}
fig = px.bar(
    country,
    x="Country",
    y="Sales",
    title="Revenue by Country",
    text_auto=".2s"
)
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Country",
    yaxis_title="Sales ($)",
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=60, b=20)
)
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.show();
```
## Row {height=300px}

```{python}
top_products = product.head(10)
fig = px.bar(
    top_products,
    x="Sales",
    y="Product",
    orientation="h",
    title="Top 10 Products by Revenue",
    text_auto=".2s"
)
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Product",
    yaxis=dict(categoryorder="total ascending"),
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=60, b=20)
)
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.show();
```
## Row {height=300px}
```{python}
top_people = salesperson.head(10)
fig = px.bar(
    top_people,
    x="Sales",
    y="Sales Person",
    orientation="h",
    title="Top 10 Salesmen by Revenue",
    text_auto=".2s"
)
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Sales Person",
    yaxis=dict(categoryorder="total ascending"),
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=60, b=20)
)
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.show();
```

## Row {height=300px}


```{python}

country_table = (
    country
    .sort_values("Sales", ascending=False)
    .assign(
        Avg_Order_Value=(country["Sales"] / country["Orders"]).round(2)
    )
    .rename(columns={
        "Sales": "Sales ($)",
        "Boxes": "Boxes Shipped",
        "Orders": "Orders",
        "Avg_Order_Value": "Avg Order Value ($)"
    })
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)

country_table.index = country_table.index + 1

country_table
```

## Row {height=600px}
## Performance by Orders
```{python}
product_table = (
    product
    .sort_values("Orders", ascending=False)
    .assign(
        Avg_Order_Value=(product["Sales"] / product["Orders"]).round(2)
    )
    .rename(columns={
        "Orders": "Orders",
        "Sales": "Sales ($)",
        "Boxes": "Boxes Shipped",
        "Avg_Order_Value": "Avg Order Value ($)"
    })
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)
product_table.index = product_table.index + 1
product_table
```
## Row {height=300px}

```{python}
summary = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": [
        "Top country by sales",
        "Top product by sales",
        "Highest revenue salesperson",
        "Average order value",
        "Total shipment volume"
    ],
    "Value": [
        top_country,
        top_product,
        salesperson.iloc[0]["Sales Person"],
        f"${avg_order:,.0f}",
        f"{total_boxes:,} boxes"
    ]
})
summary.index=summary.index + 1
summary
```
